The Elite Six

Mohammad Akbar Khan
The Author

Mohammad Akbar Khan

Known by readers as AlexbladexAkbar, I write dark fiction about broken systems, unbreakable people, and the justice that lives in the space between what the world promises and what it delivers.

The Elite Six was born from a single question: what happens when the structures meant to protect us decide that protection is inconvenient? The answer, it turns out, is a story about grief, purpose, chosen family, and the particular courage of people who answer the world's failures with action instead of bitterness.

I write for readers who want stories that feel earned — characters who carry weight, worlds that resist easy solutions, and moments of beauty that land harder because they're surrounded by darkness.

6 Elite Members
V.I Published
∞ Pages of Lore
Universe

The World of
AlexbladexAkbar

The city of Varen is beautiful the way a trap is beautiful. Towers of glass catch the light at golden hour. Street markets run along the canal every morning. And underneath all of it — a second city, built from obligations and their collectors, from files deliberately misfiled, from judges whose verdicts fit whoever could afford the tailor.

I

Varen

A city of dual natures. Polished on the surface, rotten underneath. A machine designed to ensure the powerless stay that way. Beautiful, dangerous, and alive.

II

The System

Seven honest officers in a corrupt force. Cases deliberately misfiled. Officials whose names appear on charitable donation lists while trafficking rings walk free. The enemy isn't a person — it's a structure.

III

The Elite Six

No badges. No official recognition. No desire for either. Six individuals who go where the system won't, do what it refuses to, and leave no trace except solved cases and lifted shadows.

Available Now

Current Volume

Volume One

The Weight of Windows

"The worst things that happen to us have a tendency to become either excuses or fuel. And only one of those takes you anywhere."

— Lena Ashford

Alex Blade was nineteen years old when he held a girl who was no longer breathing. Seven years later, he is twenty-six, moving through Varen's shadows with a katana called Kōri across his back and a closed case file in his hands. The system decided Lena's death was inconvenient. He decided differently.

Volume One follows the formation of the Elite Six — six damaged, gifted, extraordinary people who find each other in the margins of a city that long ago stopped pretending to be fair. This is their origin. Their weight. And the first case that forges them into something the system never anticipated.

Dark Fiction
Urban Fantasy
Vigilante Justice

The Journey Ahead

Upcoming Volumes

II
Volume Two

The Names They Gave the Dark

The reckoning deepens

The Elite Six are no longer shadows. Something in Varen has begun to notice them — and what notices them back is older and more patient than anything they've faced.

In Writing
III
Volume Three

The Architecture of Silence

When the hunters become the hunted

Every structure has a cornerstone. Find it, and the whole edifice falls. The Six discover that dismantling a corrupt system means understanding who built it — and why they're still building.

Planned
IV–VI
The Concluding Arc

Volumes Four Through Six

The complete reckoning

Three final volumes will bring every thread to its necessary end. Some windows cannot stay open. Some walls, once built, cannot be dismantled without bringing down everything around them.

Planned
The Six

Character Archive

Six broken people. Six impossible abilities. One shared conviction that the world owes its failures an answer — not bitterness, not resignation. An answer.

01 — FOUNDER
Alex Blade
The Architect

Twenty-six. Six foot two. A katana named Kōri across his back. He lost everything to the system's convenient silence at nineteen. He spent the next seven years becoming the answer to it.

Ability: Absolute Combat Precision
02 — GHOST
Kieran Vael
The Unseen

Twenty-four. Smiles constantly — it's armour he built at eight watching his mother carry things she never put down. Moves through the world without leaving a trace in human perception.

Ability: Perceptual Invisibility
03 — FORCE
Sera Lyn
Ruby Wolf

Fighter. The underground circuits of Varen know her name. Soft face, devastating eyes. She learned early that being underestimated is a tactical asset — and she has never stopped exploiting it.

Ability: Enhanced Combat Instinct
04 — IRON
Elias Morn
The Twice-Dead

Has died twice. Tells you this without drama — in the same tone he'd tell you the time. Meticulous record-keeper. He is very difficult to kill, and he has decided this is a responsibility.

Ability: Accelerated Regeneration
05 — SPARK
Davan Rell
Beautiful Disaster

Twenty-two. Youngest of the Six. Arrives in rooms rather than entering them. The kind of unpredictability that reads as recklessness until you realize it's calculated freedom.

Ability: Kinetic Energy Manipulation
06 — CIPHER
The Unnamed
The Space Between Things

He did not give his name. Arrived on an unremarkable morning and left a mark that was anything but. The most unknown of the Six — and perhaps, therefore, the most dangerous.

Ability: Unknown
Deep History

Lore & Chronicles

✦

The Prologue: The Last Warmth

A girl named Lena Ashford. Dark hair that went wavy in the rain. She collected pressed flowers in every book she read. She saw something she wasn't supposed to see — and the system decided she was an inconvenient loose end. The rain kept falling.

◆

The Halvern Resolution

The Elite Six's first major documented operation. A trafficking network running out of Varen's lower district. Four disappearances. Three of them children. The official case: closed. The Elite Six's case: resolved — completely, permanently, without trace.

⟡

The Blade Kōri

The old word for ice. Handed to Alex by Fujin Ressel — last practitioner of a dying swordsmanship school — after two years of training. The final lesson: "You have learned to be still. That is the hardest thing. Everything else is craft."

◈

The Seven Honest Officers

In a police force divided down the middle, seven officers remain uncorrupted. Alex identifies them. Every resolved case lands on their desks — packaged evidence, clean documentation, no explanation, no credit sought. Justice delivered through the cracks of a broken system.

Reader Voices

What Readers Say

★★★★★

"The prologue alone destroyed me. I had to put the book down and sit with it. Lena's philosophy — windows, not walls — has genuinely changed how I think about the people I love."

— R. Osei, Lagos
★★★★★

"Alex Blade is one of the most compellingly written protagonists I've encountered in dark fiction. The restraint in how he moves through the world — nothing wasted, nothing performed — is extraordinary."

— K. Sharma, Delhi
★★★★★

"Varen feels real in that unsettling way the best fictional cities do — like you've been there and didn't notice until the book made you look at it clearly. Kieran Vael stole my heart. His smile is a whole novel."

— A. Mensah, Accra
★★★★★

"I finished this in one sitting. Not because I couldn't put it down — because I was afraid to. Every time I stopped reading, the weight of what these characters carry followed me around my apartment."

— T. Fontaine, Paris
★★★★★

"The conversation between Alex and Marielle in The Meridian café is the best scene I've read this year. Two people who understand each other without performing it. Dark fiction at its most human."

— P. Nakamura, Tokyo
★★★★★

"Ruby Wolf. That's it. That's the review. Go read it and understand why her chapter is the one I've recommended to seventeen people since I finished the book."

— C. Adeyemi, London
